entries.value: This self-describing window shows the manifest, locale JSON files, scope and keys, fallback behavior, and validation paths used by UnityEditorLocalization.
Display language for this sample scope
Switches the display language for this scope. Clearing a scope override falls back to the global setting or the defaultLocale.

When you switch the compact menu in the upper-right corner or the display-language dropdown, Label, Button, HelpBox, Foldout, ProgressBar, DropdownField, tooltip, and placeholder text follow the translations for the same scope.
Use this area to compare the standard EditorL10nUi bindings with RegisterLocaleCallback.

This section is built with UI Toolkit from C# only, without UXML or USS. Elements with standard helpers use BindText and BindButton, while elements without dedicated helpers update through RegisterLocaleCallback.
The DropdownField label, choices, and description are rebuilt when the locale changes.
Long descriptions wrap, spacing is intentionally roomy, and the layout is designed to avoid horizontal overflow in a small window.
The manifest stores the scope, defaultLocale, and locale-table paths. Each locale JSON file only stores a list of keys and values.
Validate Catalogs checks missing or extra keys, placeholder numbers, and likely untranslated values. The translation-quality script adds terminology and length checks.
This tooltip is updated when the locale changes. The input value is not saved.
The ProgressBar title and tooltip are updated through RegisterLocaleCallback.
Calls EditorL10n.Reload and reads the manifest and locale tables again.
Calls EditorL10nValidator.ValidateAll and shows the result inside this window.
Click a button to show the operation result here.
A scope is the namespace for translation keys. Give each consumer extension a collision-resistant value.
Examples: header.title, demo.button.reload.text, details.fallback. Name keys by feature area and role, not by display order or literal UI text.
The manifest is not visible from AssetDatabase yet. Import the sample and then run Reload Catalogs.
UnityEditorLocalization tries the display locale, then parent locales, and finally falls back to the defaultLocale ja.

You can run the same catalog validation from Tools > UnityEditorLocalization > Validate Catalogs. For static translation quality checks, run validate_locale_quality.py against the sample Locales directory.
The Foldout title and tooltip also follow locale changes.
The manifest defines the sample scope, defaultLocale, and ja/en locale-table paths. Adding another language should only require manifest and JSON changes, not C# edits.
Each locale JSON file is an array of { key, value } entries. Placeholders use numbered tokens such as {0} and {1}, and every locale must keep the same placeholder-number set.
Separating scope and key prevents translation-key collisions when multiple consumer Editor extensions are installed in one Unity project.
Fallback tries parent locales in order, such as es-419 to es, and then the defaultLocale. This sample uses ja as the defaultLocale.
skills/editor-localization-translation-quality/ contains translation review guidance and validate_locale_quality.py. The same checks can be applied to consumer locale sets.
